John Lennon or Martin Luther King Jr? Has a homeless ever berated you with spoiled breath from their shit stained skivvies? Does it truly bother you?
I dont wish to diminish the terrible deeds these monstrous angels have done, have you ever done wrong? Killing a spider just because its a norm isnt less terrible, we might be worth more to us but to the universe, its all perfect. 
We need to celebrate art and let it be, let the police do their job but damn Spacey is a good actor...
Neil Gaiman has saved so many lives with his art, karma is instant and he will pay, but like Death says, she sees us all the same eventually...
I love you Neil, I love you who judge, i love you who have been assaulted by those in power and you will one day thank that day for making you more than you ever thought possible. 
If we found a way to hold space for those that hurt, so they could heal, we would be superhuman.

Even though Sandman is based of a very clear Christian and European mythos, it's interesting to note that in Vishnu Purana in Hindu Mythology life is termed as 
 
"Vishnu's Dream" -- Vishnu is the Preserver.

I just have to give credit where credit is due: The Sandman on Netflix is hands down one of the most beautifully edited and visually crafted series I’ve ever seen. Especially in Season 2 you can feel how much care, detail & emotional intuition went into every frame. The entire atmosphere is truly dreamlike and I mean that literally. The show captures the logic, the surreal flow and the emotional texture of real dreams in a way that’s almost uncanny. That’s not easy to pull off. It takes serious artistic sensitivity and a deep understanding of visuals, timing and mood. 
Massive respect to everyone involved …this is true cinematic artistry!

comment_text:
Unfortunately, to make the TV series family-friendly, they were not faithful to the comic book illustrations... I will never get tired of saying this, but censorship on art is stupid.

I was afraid they would censor art and use actors who don't adequately portray the eternals... and unfortunately I was right... Dream and Desire for now are the only actors who can properly portray their roles and are very good at it, Delirium for now is good, but I think she needs more mind tripping animation to help her interpretation.

Here are some things that need to be changed:

1) Death: In the comic she appears as an attractive, composed and bold young gothic woman, but unfortunately the actress has none of that.

2) Despair: A short, obese woman with grey skin and uneven teeth, constantly naked, who has a hooked ring and uses it to constantly cut her skin and injure herself, she is also perpetually naked.... Here you can clearly see how much they have censored the art.

3) Destiny: The oldest of the Eternals, he is supposed to be old and blind in the comics.... but in the TV series he is not, another stupid censorship.

I love Sandman since I first read it when I was 13, I reread all 10 volumes every year since then. And I absolutely loved the series, especially casting Tom as Dream and Gwendolyn as Lucifer.

I was shocked when I read about Neil Gaiman's scandals, because he is the last person I would expect to be accused in such a violence. If he truly is guilty then he must take all responsibility for it, including punishment. but I really wish that accusations not to be true. His books have a special place in my heart.

The Sandman Season 2 returns with Volume 1 on July 3 and Volume 2 on July 24.
Wednesday Season 2 returns with Volume 1 on August 6 and Volume 2 on September 3.

comment_text:
In my time on the earth, I've seen people lie. I've seen them lie horribly, and in unison. I've seen men do it. I've seen women do it. Speak not about what you know nothing of. The truth sorts itself out in time. Acting as if you know the score, when you in fact know nothing of the sort, is hardly worthy of the energy I'm expending typing this comment. 

I know this... Neil wrote a piece of story in 'The Wake' that ended up in a dream, as well as an out of body experience that I had, prior to ever reading the story. I asked him about this once, and he told me I wasn't the only person who had reported such strange experiences that correlated with his writing of Sandman. Everyone here who has spoken negatively of this man since the allegations (key word "alleged") might do well to remember the magick this man has conjured for the world to devour. Many of you are the same ones who spoke so highly of his inclusive choices and character prior to the current prospective narrative. Many of you are trolls who feed upon lies.. So hellbent upon being self-righteous, you've lost your wit (if you ever had it), and you've lost your ability to exercise judgement within the realm of patience and careful consideration. Conclusion jumpers galore. 

I'll say this.. I stood once in a court of law, all the cards on my side, all the truth on my side, with a heap of burning hot coal on the side of the other, and I lost that case. A case where lies were lifted up and bias had the final word. My point? Being quick to judgement and following a particular crowd doesn't make you correct. And often times, neither does a verdict. The truth knows itself, and it knows of who it is known. And I do declare, none of the members of the pseudo judge and jury here are within that circle. 

Check yourselves.

Thank you for the magick, Neil. May the truth prevail 🎩

comment_text: The first season adapted the first 20 issues or so, with some notable stuff missing (the Shakespeare stuff, for one). So we're supposed to have the remaining ~55 issues crammed into one season? This can't possibly work unless they cut A LOT of things. A Game of You, The World's End and Fables are probably not going to feature; my guess is that we'll get mostly Season of Mists and a shortened or mashed up version of Brief Lives and The Kindly Ones. What a shame: I can almost garantee that this season will be far worse than the first one because there's no way they're going to keep the level of quality by fitting so much in just one season.
